title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup · Language Lens: Pokedex Go Request Context
summary: Each command creates a new client, uses context.Background inside dependencies, ignores non-2xx status, decodes unbounded bodies, and forgets to close responses. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go request context increment in reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Create requests with caller-owned context and derive request deadlines without hiding cancellation behind Background.
Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
